title: The High School Reunion (2): The Gang's Revenge

jokes.text: Mac realizes fake tickets, calls Adriano 'Ronnie Rat strikes again'
jokes.setup: Mac discovers they have fake reunion tickets
jokes.punchline: Immediately blames wrong person with confident authority
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac - confidently wrong about everything while trying to sound street smart

jokes.text: Dennis lamenting sitting alone with 'my sister, the Aluminum Monster'
jokes.setup: Dennis bemoans his fallen social status
jokes.punchline: Casually refers to sister by her humiliating nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis narcissism - more concerned about his image than his sister's feelings

jokes.text: Dee claiming she was also 'on the mountaintop'
jokes.setup: Dennis talks about being king of the mountaintop
jokes.punchline: Dee delusionally claims equal status despite being 'Aluminum Monster'
jokes.explanation: Classic Dee delusion - rewriting history to inflate her own importance

jokes.text: Fatty Magoo! Right? Everybody, Fatty Magoo. Honk!
jokes.setup: Dee tries to reclaim social status by bullying someone with an old nickname
jokes.punchline: The target is no longer fat, making Dee look pathetic and cruel
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Dee's desperation to reclaim popularity leads to obvious cruelty. The failed bullying attempt lands because it's so pathetically out of touch.

jokes.text: Dennis mockingly speaking gibberish at Ingrid
jokes.setup: Ingrid explains she's on alumni association
jokes.punchline: Dennis responds with childish mocking sounds
jokes.explanation: Pathetic regression to high school behavior, showing Dennis hasn't matured

jokes.text: She's not fat anymore. That doesn't really play.
jokes.setup: Dee's aggressive bullying of 'Fatty Magoo'
jokes.punchline: Dennis's clinical assessment that the bit isn't working
jokes.explanation: Dennis treating bullying like a comedy bit that's bombing is perfectly in character and economically delivered.

jokes.text: 'Cause I'll see it on the sides, and I'll see it in the thighs. Still fat to me, still fat to you, Fatty Magoo...
jokes.setup: Being told the fat joke doesn't work anymore
jokes.punchline: Dee doubles down with increasingly desperate body-shaming
jokes.explanation: Dee's pathetic insistence on continuing a failed bit shows her complete lack of self-awareness. The escalation makes it more uncomfortable.

jokes.text: That seemed pathetic and mean.
jokes.setup: Dee's failed bullying attempt
jokes.punchline: Dennis stating the obvious about their behavior with complete sincerity
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor where the show acknowledges its characters' awfulness through Dennis's assessment

jokes.text: That felt very forced. Yeah, 'cause she's so pretty these days.
jokes.setup: Dee's failed bullying attempt
jokes.punchline: The characters acknowledge how forced and pathetic it was
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the failed joke works as self-aware cringe comedy typical of the show's style.

jokes.text: I'm gonna bang Tim Murphy's black wife.
jokes.setup: Need for revenge against high school enemies
jokes.punchline: Dennis's plan is both racist and completely misguided about what would constitute revenge
jokes.explanation: Dennis's casual racism and complete misunderstanding of effective revenge is in character but uncomfortable.

jokes.text: I will bang Tim Murphy to get back at Adriano.
jokes.setup: Dennis's revenge plan involving sex
jokes.punchline: Dee's plan makes even less logical sense than Dennis's
jokes.explanation: Dee's plan is even more illogical than Dennis's, showing their shared narcissism and poor planning.

jokes.text: The problem is, I don't think Tim Murphy would ever sleep with you.
jokes.setup: Dee's plan to seduce Tim Murphy
jokes.punchline: Dennis's brutal assessment of her chances
jokes.explanation: Dennis's casual cruelty to his sister delivered matter-of-factly is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Mac: 'It was a massacre'
jokes.setup: Charlie asks what happened to them
jokes.punchline: Mac delivers dramatic war metaphor for social embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Mac's tendency to dramatize minor social slights with military language

jokes.text: Charlie mentions 'the freight train'
jokes.setup: Gang needs revenge plan
jokes.punchline: Charlie suggests reforming their pathetic high school 'gang'
jokes.explanation: Sets up the ridiculous 'freight train' storyline and their delusions about past coolness

jokes.text: Our conductor's insane. Our cargo is pain. Freight train!
jokes.setup: Discussion of their old high school gang
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous motto that tries to sound tough but is just silly
jokes.explanation: The rhyming motto is perfectly stupid for their fake tough-guy gang, combining wordplay with absurdity.

jokes.text: If anything, he'll swoop in at the very end, grab the drunkest girl he can, take her home, bang her. Ha, Schmitty's so cool.
jokes.setup: Discussion of their cool friend Schmitty
jokes.punchline: Mac admires predatory behavior as 'cool'
jokes.explanation: Mac's admiration for clearly predatory behavior reveals his warped value system.

jokes.text: I've tacked on mass. Really? I've tacked on lots of mass, yes.
jokes.setup: Being called fat
jokes.punchline: Mac's delusional insistence he's gained muscle mass
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac delusion about his obvious weight gain. The repetition and certainty make it funnier.

jokes.text: Was he joking when he cut his family into little pieces and ate them for Christmas dinner?
jokes.setup: Defending Psycho Pete's dark sense of humor
jokes.punchline: Reveal of genuinely horrific cannibalistic murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from 'dark sense of humor' to actual cannibalism. The casual delivery makes it more shocking.

jokes.text: I specialize in bird law, so I don't want to mess around...
jokes.setup: Discussion of legal defense strategy
jokes.punchline: Charlie's completely made-up legal specialty
jokes.explanation: Charlie's invented expertise in 'bird law' is perfectly absurd and in character. Highly quotable nonsense.

jokes.text: I'd rather get blasted in the balls by a battering ram than have to have a conversation with the guy.
jokes.setup: Discussing how boring Tim Murphy is
jokes.punchline: Extremely violent metaphor for mild social discomfort
jokes.explanation: Dennis's dramatic overstatement about mild social annoyance is typical of his narcissistic worldview.

jokes.text: Baby's got back, Christie. Baby's got back.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to seduce Tim's wife
jokes.punchline: Using a dated pop culture reference as seduction
jokes.explanation: Dennis's awkward seduction attempt using outdated references shows his social incompetence.

jokes.text: Because you're gay.
jokes.setup: Dennis asking why she wouldn't want to have sex with him
jokes.punchline: Christie's matter-of-fact assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - two words that deflate Dennis's entire seduction attempt. The directness is brutal.

jokes.text: Dude, you're wearing makeup. Yeah, I'm wearing a little bit of makeup. Who-who doesn't... And a girdle.
jokes.setup: Dennis denying he's gay
jokes.punchline: Evidence keeps piling up against his denial
jokes.explanation: Dennis's weak justifications get progressively worse as more evidence mounts.

jokes.text: I was demonstrating value... then I engaged you physically... nurturing your dependence... neglect you emotionally. That's what I do, Christie.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to explain his seduction technique
jokes.punchline: He describes psychological manipulation like a business plan
jokes.explanation: Dennis's clinical description of emotional manipulation as a systematic process is both hilarious and horrifying. Classic character work.

jokes.text: I am the king of the mountaintop! I reign supreme over everyone in this school. I am the golden god of this place. I reign supreme. I!
jokes.setup: Dennis's rejection leading to a breakdown
jokes.punchline: His completely unhinged proclamation of superiority
jokes.explanation: Iconic Dennis meltdown moment, became a major meme and character-defining scene

jokes.text: I am the golden god of this place. I reign supreme. I!
jokes.setup: Dennis's mounting frustration with rejection
jokes.punchline: Complete narcissistic meltdown in public
jokes.explanation: Dennis's complete breakdown into megalomania is perfectly in character. The awkward silence after makes it funnier.

jokes.text: [awkward silence as music and chatter resume]
jokes.setup: Dennis's dramatic 'golden god' declaration
jokes.punchline: Everyone just ignores his meltdown and continues the party
jokes.explanation: The complete indifference to Dennis's dramatic moment is the perfect punchline. The silence speaks volumes.

jokes.text: I'm feeling very, very lucky to have my asshole ripped in half like tissue paper by my father and his very good friends.
jokes.setup: Dee being sarcastic about the freight train initiation
jokes.punchline: Graphic description of apparent sexual assault (but it's actually just the initiation)
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about the initiation hazing, but the imagery is uncomfortably graphic even for this show.

jokes.text: Tools! Uh, duct tape, zip ties and gloves! I have to have my tools!
jokes.setup: Dennis grabbing items from his car for revenge
jokes.punchline: The items sound like serial killer supplies
jokes.explanation: Dennis's 'tools' revelation is genuinely disturbing but delivered with such manic energy it becomes darkly comic.

jokes.text: It's fetish, it's fetish shit! I-I-I-I like to bind... I like to be bound.
jokes.setup: Questions about why he has restraints in his car
jokes.punchline: Dennis's panicked sexual confession
jokes.explanation: Dennis's desperate attempt to explain away the serial killer supplies with TMI about his sex life is perfectly uncomfortable.

jokes.text: No, we don't do the toot-toots... We're a train. Why wouldn't we toot-toot?
jokes.setup: The train sound effects
jokes.punchline: Charlie's logical confusion about train sound protocol
jokes.explanation: Charlie's innocent logic applied to their ridiculous situation

jokes.text: You were never that cool to begin with. You would just come around saying weird shit about being a golden god or some other insane crap.
jokes.setup: Dennis's delusions about his high school popularity
jokes.punchline: Reality check that he was always weird, not cool
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Dennis's megalomaniacal outburst, revealing he's always been this delusional.

jokes.text: I didn't sleep with your prom date. Ronald McDonald slept with your prom date.
jokes.setup: Dennis accusing Tim of sleeping with his prom date
jokes.punchline: It was actually Mac (nicknamed Ronald McDonald) who did it
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that shifts blame and reveals Mac's nickname. The betrayal by his own friend is classic.

jokes.text: This is mass! Don't make it about my weight!
jokes.setup: Being called fat during the fight
jokes.punchline: Mac's continued delusion about his weight gain
jokes.explanation: Mac maintaining his delusion even during a physical altercation is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: If you're dealt a bunch of lemons, you got to take those lemons and stuff them down somebody's throat until they see yellow.
jokes.setup: Frank giving a motivational speech
jokes.punchline: Completely mangled version of 'when life gives you lemons'
jokes.explanation: Frank's violent twist on the classic saying is perfectly absurd and in character. The 'see yellow' detail makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Frank's incoherent motivational speech about lemons
jokes.setup: Gang needs motivation after total defeat
jokes.punchline: Frank delivers nonsensical mashup of motivational clichés
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank moment - attempting wisdom but producing word salad

jokes.text: Well, the thing of it is, is that, uh, there's the lemon stuff. Right. And then you got Mac, who's a rat. And Dee's body brace. And, uh, and that's the thing of it, and... It's good.
jokes.setup: Frank was asked what the one thing left to do is
jokes.punchline: Frank completely loses his train of thought and rambles nonsensically
jokes.explanation: Frank's complete cognitive breakdown after delivering a coherent speech is typical of his character's unpredictability.

jokes.text: Plan B is a dance routine. It's a goddamned dance routine.
jokes.setup: Plan B being discussed in hushed, serious tones
jokes.punchline: The big secret plan is just a dance routine
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - the serious buildup makes the mundane reality funnier. Dennis's exasperation sells it.

jokes.text: That's how they solved their problems-- through dance. Then, all of a sudden, we stopped dancing... Then, later on, kids wore trench coats and shot each other in school, and that's not cool.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining the importance of dance
jokes.punchline: Blaming school shootings on the decline of dancing
jokes.explanation: Mac's completely absurd causation between dancing and violence is darkly funny but feels a bit forced.

jokes.text: They also give each other blow jobs. They got these things called rainbow parties. No, Charlie.
jokes.setup: Mac's speech about modern kids dancing
jokes.punchline: Charlie derails with inappropriate sexual information
jokes.explanation: Charlie's inappropriate interjection is perfectly in character but disrupts the flow of Mac's already absurd speech.

jokes.text: [The gang performs an elaborate dance routine that completely bombs]
jokes.setup: The gang's elaborate plan to win over the reunion through dance
jokes.punchline: Their serious, elaborate routine is completely ignored and rejected
jokes.explanation: The extended visual gag of their elaborate dance being completely rejected is peak cringe comedy. The effort makes the failure funnier.

jokes.text: [Frank vomiting sounds]
jokes.setup: The failed dance performance
jokes.punchline: Frank literally vomits from the exertion/humiliation
jokes.explanation: Frank vomiting is the perfect gross-out capper to their humiliation, though it's somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: Tim's after party invitation excluding them
jokes.setup: Dance performance ends
jokes.punchline: Tim publicly excludes them while inviting everyone else
jokes.explanation: Final humiliation - public exclusion after their failed performance

jokes.text: I don't understand how that didn't blow people's minds, though.
jokes.setup: The dance routine completely bombing
jokes.punchline: Charlie's genuine confusion about why it failed
jokes.explanation: Charlie's complete lack of self-awareness about their terrible performance is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I'm gonna bang the next person who talks to me. So who's it gonna be?
jokes.setup: Dee's drunken desperation for validation
jokes.punchline: Setting herself up for humiliation by announcing her low standards
jokes.explanation: Dee's desperate announcement sets up her own humiliation perfectly, which is classic for her character.

jokes.text: Hey, Schmitty, excuse me. How you doing, having fun? ... Oh, do you want to have sex with me? Yes, I do. Well, there you go.
jokes.setup: Dee's drunken proposition to the next person who talks to her
jokes.punchline: It's Schmitty, who immediately accepts, fulfilling the earlier prediction
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the earlier setup about Schmitty's MO. The casualness of the transaction makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Swooped in and grabbed the most disgusting girl here.
jokes.setup: Watching Schmitty leave with Dee
jokes.punchline: Mac's admiring assessment that calls Dee disgusting
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that both completes the prediction and insults Dee. Mac's admiration makes it worse.
